posture: Quiet Thursday scan — no confirmable new, in-scope innovation signal surfaced in the last 24 hours. The sector's active frontiers (FOGO/circular economy, AI-assisted development assessment, smart communities) remain warm but produced no fresh, in-window council announcement today; the strongest recent 'adopt' material remains the 2026 National Awards for Local Government already logged on 29 June.
summary: Empty day. A full daily scan of the §1a-§1d free sources (AU council & sector press, AU industry verticals, overseas equal-weight outlets and the tertiary/research institutes) returned no item that was simultaneously (a) published in the last 24 hours, (b) in scope per the §3b Australian-council-function filter, and (c) not already covered in the previous 3 days. Recurring items were again reviewed and excluded as out-of-window, out-of-scope or duplicative: the NSW statewide FOGO rollout (source-separated organics for supermarkets/institutions/hospitality from 1 July 2026; household mandate to 2030) is a rollout date rather than a fresh innovation signal; Hawkesbury City Council's ARC Ento Tech black-soldier-fly waste-to-resource trial is a January 2026 commencement (prior-period background); the NSW $5.6m AI-in-planning Early Adopter trial across 16 councils and the Bathurst Regional Council 4D digital twin are previously-known rollouts with no fresh dated development today. AU waste/roads trade coverage (Waste 2026 conference wrap, Austroads recycled-road framework, Alex Fraser Queensland quarry registration) was general sector commentary or out-of-window. Overseas material (Bloomberg CityLab 2026 / Mayors Challenge 2025-26 winners, Mayors AI Forum, Harvard Ash Center municipal-innovation content, LGIU/ANZSOG output) was out-of-window, network/policy content, or already logged as background rather than a fresh, council-transferable signal. A NSW police-surveillance-drone council trial surfaced but is out of scope per §3b (law enforcement). Today is a Thursday, so the §1e 50-council watchlist deep scan (Saturdays only) was not run. No paywalled items were logged; where a paid title was the only source it was held to headline + standfirst and not used. Sections are published empty per §6.
